summary: Pass 1 performed a codebase analysis and produced an ANALYSIS.md report in the project root. The critical finding is that this is a terminal CLI application built with Ink (React for terminals) and `ink-text-input`, NOT a web frontend — this fundamentally constrains what UI libraries and patterns can be used in downstream tasks. The primary input component is `PromptInput` at `src/cli/ui/PromptInput.tsx`, a minimal ~50-line wrapper around `ink-text-input` that adds a prompt character, loading spinner, and trim-on-submit behavior. All behavioral complexity — mode switching, input queuing, slash commands, and pipeline interaction — resides in the parent `App` component's state machine, not in the input wrapper itself. The input component serves triple duty: starting workflows, answering pipeline questions, and accepting slash commands. The ANALYSIS.md report covers tech stack, dependencies, input component deep-dive, usage mapping with data flow diagrams, file dependency graph, and key observations.
unresolvedIssues: ANALYSIS.md was created in the project root rather than a docs/ directory — should be moved or treated as a transient artifact
The completeness and accuracy of the ANALYSIS.md file contents could not be fully verified from the ingest output alone
Any downstream tasks expecting a web frontend stack need to be re-scoped for a terminal CLI (Ink) environment — web-oriented UI libraries will not work
Feature enhancements to input behavior may require modifying the parent App component's state machine rather than PromptInput itself, which adds complexity
contextForNextPass:
KEY ARCHITECTURE: This is a terminal CLI app using Ink (React for terminals), NOT a web app. The npm package `ink-text-input` is the sole input library. 

PRIMARY INPUT COMPONENT: `src/cli/ui/PromptInput.tsx` — a ~50-line wrapper around `ink-text-input`. It is minimal: adds prompt character, loading spinner, trim-on-submit. 

BEHAVIORAL COMPLEXITY LOCATION: The parent `App` component (likely `src/cli/ui/App.tsx` or similar) contains the state machine that controls mode switching, input queuing, slash commands, and pipeline interaction. To change input behavior, you likely need to modify App, not just PromptInput.

INPUT SERVES TRIPLE DUTY: (1) starting workflows, (2) answering pipeline questions, (3) accepting slash commands — all through the same PromptInput component, differentiated by App's state machine.

CONSTRAINT: Any plugin or component additions must be compatible with the terminal/Ink environment. Standard web React components and DOM-based libraries cannot be used.

REPORT LOCATION: `ANALYSIS.md` in project root contains the full detailed analysis.
